title: Accidental Confiscation
description: Accidental confiscation is the risk that a poorly designed soft fork makes previously spendable coins effectively unspendable. It is most acute for reserved upgrade features, presigned transactions, and hidden committed scripts whose future constraints may change.

resources.title: Bitcoin Optech Topic: Accidental Confiscation
resources.notes: Canonical source for the risk model, including reserved upgrade features, presigned transactions, and committed scripts.

resources.title: Bitcoin Optech Topic: Soft Fork Activation
resources.notes: Broader activation context for why new consensus restrictions can create coordination and safety failures.

resources.title: Bitcoin Optech Topic: Consensus Cleanup Soft Fork
resources.notes: Concrete example of upgrade discussions where old feature usage and backward compatibility become spendability concerns.

resources.title: The Blocksize War
resources.notes: Long-form history of why activation design, backward compatibility, and consensus safety have social and economic consequences.
